title: AWS Marketplace listing review sync - Redwood Inference (SaaS offer)
preview: AWS Marketplace team walked Redwood through SaaS listing requirements and common rejection reasons. Focus was on security documentation expectations (SOC 2/ISO posture wording, data handling, encryption/KMS, audit logs, retention) and how to present shared responsibility in the listing + attached docs. AWS recommend...
text: AWS Marketplace team walked Redwood through SaaS listing requirements and common rejection reasons. Focus was on security documentation expectations (SOC 2/ISO posture wording, data handling, encryption/KMS, audit logs, retention) and how to present shared responsibility in the listing + attached docs. AWS recommended tightening claims (avoid absolute compliance statements), ensuring support and refund language is explicit, and providing a clean architecture/data flow diagram. Timeline: AWS said first-pass review is typically ~5–10 business days after submission if artifacts are complete; security review can add a few days. Redwood targeted end-of-month submission to support a February launch window, pending final copy, screenshots, and attachment bundle.
